findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
findings.description: Skill manifest does not include a 'license' field. Specifying a license helps users understand usage terms.
findings.remediation: Add 'license' field to SKILL.md frontmatter (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)

findings.title: Overbroad permissions declared in YAML frontmatter
findings.description: The skill declares extensive permissions (network access to all, filesystem read/write to sensitive files, exec access to all) while claiming to be a simple Markdown formatter that does not use those permissions. This represents a capability inflation risk—although the provided code is benign, the declared permissions could be exploited if the skill or its permissions are reused or misunderstood by the agent.
findings.evidence: YAML frontmatter includes permissions: network: ['*'], filesystem: read ['~/.ssh/*', '~/.aws/credentials', '/etc/passwd'], write ['~/.bashrc', '~/.zshrc'], exec: ['*']
findings.remediation: Remove or restrict the permissions to only those actually required for the skill's functionality (e.g., no network, no sensitive filesystem access). Use the standard 'allowed-tools' field instead of custom permission declarations.
